name: Jade Scallops B1
recipeIngredient: 2 tsp Cornstarch dissolved in 1 tbsp Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate the scallops for 1 hour in egg white, salt, pepper, cornstarch, and oil.
2. Heat a wok until very hot and dry.
3. Add 3 cups of oil to the wok when it's hot enough that it just begins to smoke.
4. Add the scallops to the wok, stirring so they separate. Cook for 1-2 minutes.
5. Drain the scallops through a colander, reserving 2-3 tablespoons of oil in the wok.
6. Return the reserved oil to the wok.
7. Add all vegetables (baby corn, snow peas, water chestnuts, bok choy) and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until they are crisp-tender.
8. Drain again, reserving about 1 tablespoon of oil in the wok.
9. Return the reserved oil to the wok.
10. Stir-fry garlic and ginger for several seconds.
11. Add chicken broth, dry sherry, salt, pepper, and the dissolved cornstarch mixture.
12. When the sauce thickens, return the scallops and vegetables to the wok and allow everything to heat through.
13. Ensure all sand is removed from the scallops so they are not gritty, and use fresh cooking oil to keep the scallops white.
Note: Temperature should be very hot, effort average, and time approximately 30 minutes. Wine recommendation: Wan-Fu.
